question: [File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take / Midnight.settlementFee (TTM >= 360 days)] Can a taker take a SetterRatifier-authorized offer with TTM > 360 days (market maturity far in the future), causing settlementFee to return cbp6 * CBP (the 360d breakpoint fee), and if the feeSetter subsequently lowers cbp6 before the take, the fee is underclaimed relative to what the maker expected when registering the root for a long-dated market? Preconditions: market maturity is > 360 days from now; maker registers root expecting cbp6 fee; feeSetter lowers cbp6; taker takes. Call sequence: setIsRootRatified -> setMarketSettlementFee(id, 6, lowerFee) -> take(offer, ...). Invariant tested: settlement fee for TTM >= 360 days must equal cbp6 * CBP at time of take. Scoped impact: settlement fee underclaimed for long-dated markets after fee reduction. Proof idea: unit test asserting settlementFee(id, 361 days) == cbp6 * CBP after fee change.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take / Midnight._updatePosition (conditional call)] Can a taker, by taking a buy offer where the buyer has existing debt exactly equal to units (buyerPos.debt == units), cause the condition (hasCredit(id, buyer) || units > buyerPos.debt) to be false (no credit, units not > debt), skipping _updatePosition for the buyer, and then the buyer's debt is reduced to zero (buyerPos.debt -= units - buyerCreditIncrease = units - 0 = units), leaving the buyer with zero debt and zero credit but a stale lastAccrual, causing future continuous fee accrual to be computed from the wrong baseline? Preconditions: buyer has debt == units and no credit; market has non-zero continuousFee; time has elapsed since buyer's lastAccrual. Call sequence: setIsRootRatified -> take(buyOffer, ..., units == buyerDebt). Invariant tested: lastAccrual must be updated whenever a position is modified. Scoped impact: continuous fee underclaimed due to stale lastAccrual after debt repayment via take. Proof idea: unit test asserting lastAccrual is updated after take that reduces debt to zero.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.claimContinuousFee / Midnight.withdraw] Can a lender cause continuous fees to be unclaimable by calling withdraw() for all their credit immediately after a take (before updatePosition is called), causing withdrawable to decrease by the full credit amount while continuousFeeCredit has not yet been updated with the accrued fee, and then when updatePosition is called, continuousFeeCredit increases but withdrawable is already depleted, making claimContinuousFee revert? Preconditions: lender has credit; time has elapsed; lender calls withdraw before updatePosition; continuousFeeCredit > withdrawable after updatePosition. Call sequence: setIsRootRatified -> take -> withdraw(credit) -> updatePosition [continuousFeeCredit increases] -> claimContinuousFee [reverts]. Invariant tested: claimContinuousFee(continuousFeeCredit) must succeed after any sequence of withdrawals and updatePositions. Scoped impact: continuous fee unclaimable after withdrawal before accrual. Proof idea: unit test with withdraw before updatePosition asserting claimContinuousFee succeeds.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take / settlement fee (buy vs sell rounding asymmetry)] Can a taker exploit the asymmetry between buy offer rounding (mulDivDown for both buyerAssets and sellerAssets) and sell offer rounding (mulDivUp for both), causing the settlement fee (buyerAssets - sellerAssets) to differ between equivalent buy and sell offers at the same tick and units, resulting in inconsistent fee collection depending on offer direction? Preconditions: two equivalent offers (same tick, units) but one buy and one sell; settlement fee is non-zero. Call sequence: setIsRootRatified(maker1, root1, true) -> take(buyOffer, ...) -> setIsRootRatified(maker2, root2, true) -> take(sellOffer, ...). Invariant tested: settlement fee collected must be symmetric for equivalent buy and sell offers. Scoped impact: settlement fee overclaimed or underclaimed depending on offer direction. Proof idea: differential test comparing fee collected for buy vs sell offers at same tick and units.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take / Midnight.settlementFee (interpolation with equal breakpoints)] Can a taker cause the settlement fee interpolation to return an incorrect value when feeLower == feeUpper (flat fee across an interval), specifically when the interpolation formula (feeLower * (end - timeToMaturity) + feeUpper * (timeToMaturity - start)) / (end - start) simplifies to feeLower, but due to integer arithmetic, returns feeLower - 1 for certain TTM values, underclaiming the fee? Preconditions: feeLower == feeUpper for a given interval; TTM chosen within that interval. Call sequence: setIsRootRatified -> take(offer, ...) at various TTM values within the flat interval. Invariant tested: if feeLower == feeUpper, settlementFee must equal feeLower for all TTM in the interval. Scoped impact: settlement fee underclaimed by 1 unit due to integer division. Proof idea: fuzz TTM within flat-fee intervals and assert fee == feeLower.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take / Midnight._updatePosition (seller with credit and debt)] Can a taker, by taking a sell offer where the seller has both credit and debt (unusual but possible via prior takes), cause _updatePosition to be called for the seller (hasCredit == true), accruing continuous fees on the seller's credit, while the take simultaneously increases the seller's debt, causing the continuous fee to be computed on the pre-take credit but charged against the post-take position, resulting in fee mis-accounting? Preconditions: seller has both credit and debt; market has non-zero continuousFee; time has elapsed. Call sequence: setIsRootRatified -> take(buyOffer, seller as taker) [seller gains credit] -> take(sellOffer, seller as maker) [seller has credit and debt]. Invariant tested: continuous fee accrual must be based on credit at the start of the take, not after position updates. Scoped impact: continuous fee overclaimed or underclaimed when seller has mixed credit/debt. Proof idea: unit test with seller having credit and debt, asserting fee accrual is correct.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take / claimableSettlementFee (token-level aggregation)] Can a market creator create a market with a loanToken that is the same as an existing high-volume market, and by taking offers in the new market, cause claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] to aggregate fees from both markets, allowing the feeClaimer to claim fees that were contributed by the high-volume market while attributing them to the new market, effectively overclaiming from one market's lenders? Preconditions: two markets share the same loanToken; both have non-zero settlement fees; feeClaimer claims total aggregated fees. Call sequence: touchMarket(market1) -> take(offer1, ...) -> touchMarket(market2) -> take(offer2, ...) -> claimSettlementFee(loanToken, totalFees, receiver). Invariant tested: claimableSettlementFee[token] must be claimable without affecting lender withdrawable balances. Scoped impact: settlement fee overclaimed; solvency invariant at risk. Proof idea: invariant test asserting balance[token] >= sum(withdrawable[allMarkets]) + claimableSettlementFee[token].

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take / buyerPendingFeeIncrease (partial debt repayment)] Can a taker, by taking a buy offer where the buyer has existing debt D and units > D (so buyerCreditIncrease = units - D > 0), cause buyerPendingFeeIncrease to be computed on only the credit increase portion (units - D) rather than the full units, while the debt repayment portion (D units) pays no continuous fee, resulting in the continuous fee being underclaimed relative to the full units taken? Preconditions: buyer has existing debt D; units > D; market has non-zero continuousFee. Call sequence: setIsRootRatified -> take(buyOffer, ..., units > buyerDebt). Invariant tested: continuous fee must be charged on all new credit created, not just the net credit increase. Scoped impact: continuous fee underclaimed when buyer has existing debt. Proof idea: fuzz (debt, units, continuousFee, TTM) and assert pendingFeeIncrease == (units - debt) * continuousFee * TTM / WAD.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take / Midnight.settlementFee (market not yet created)] Can a taker call take() with an offer whose market has not been touched yet, causing touchMarket() to be called inside take(), which initializes the market with the current defaultSettlementFeeCbp values, and if the feeSetter changed the defaults between root registration and take, the market is initialized with different fees than the maker expected, causing fee mis-accounting from the first take? Preconditions: market not yet created; maker registers root for offers in this market; feeSetter changes defaultSettlementFee; taker takes (triggering touchMarket). Call sequence: setIsRootRatified -> setDefaultSettlementFee(loanToken, index, newFee) -> take(offer, ...) [touchMarket called inside]. Invariant tested: market fees at creation must match the fees expected by the maker at root registration time. Scoped impact: settlement fee overclaimed or underclaimed on first take due to fee change before market creation. Proof idea: unit test asserting market fees after touchMarket match defaultSettlementFee at take time.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight._updatePosition / continuousFeeCredit (post-slash)] Can a lender's continuous fee be overclaimed after bad debt socialization if the postSlashPendingFee calculation uses mulDivDown(credit - postSlashCredit, credit) to reduce pendingFee, but the slash reduces credit more than pendingFee proportionally, causing postSlashPendingFee > postSlashCredit, violating the invariant that pendingFee <= credit, and causing subsequent fee accrual to be computed on an inflated pendingFee? Preconditions: market has realized bad debt (lossFactor > 0); lender has credit and pendingFee; updatePosition called after slash. Call sequence: setIsRootRatified -> take -> liquidate (bad debt) -> updatePosition. Invariant tested: postSlashPendingFee <= postSlashCredit at all times. Scoped impact: continuous fee overclaimed after bad debt due to pendingFee > credit. Proof idea: fuzz (credit, pendingFee, lossFactor) and assert postSlashPendingFee <= postSlashCredit.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take / Midnight.settlementFee (tick == 0)] Can a taker take a SetterRatifier-authorized sell offer at tick == 0 (offerPrice = tickToPrice(0) ≈ 0), causing sellerPrice = offerPrice = ~0 and buyerPrice = offerPrice + settlementFee = settlementFee, so buyerAssets = units.mulDivUp(settlementFee, WAD) and sellerAssets = units.mulDivUp(~0, WAD) ≈ 0, meaning the entire buyer payment goes to the protocol as settlement fee with the seller receiving nothing, effectively charging the wrong side (buyer pays all, seller gets nothing)? Preconditions: offer.buy == false; offer.tick == 0; settlement fee is non-zero. Call sequence: setIsRootRatified -> take(sellOffer, tick=0, ...). Invariant tested: sellerAssets must be >= some minimum relative to buyerAssets; fee must not exceed buyerAssets. Scoped impact: settlement fee overclaimed; seller receives zero assets. Proof idea: unit test at tick=0 asserting sellerAssets > 0 when units > 0.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take / Midnight.settlementFee (buy offer, offerPrice < settlementFee)] Can a taker take a SetterRatifier-authorized buy offer where offerPrice < settlementFee, causing sellerPrice = offerPrice - settlementFee to underflow (revert in Solidity 0.8), effectively making the offer untakeable and the maker's registered root useless, freezing the maker's liquidity? Preconditions: offer.buy == true; offer.tick is low (low offerPrice); settlement fee is raised by feeSetter to exceed offerPrice. Call sequence: setIsRootRatified -> setMarketSettlementFee(id, index, highFee) -> take(buyOffer, lowTick, ...) [reverts]. Invariant tested: take must not revert for valid offers due to fee changes after root registration. Scoped impact: settlement fee change makes offer untakeable; maker's liquidity frozen. Proof idea: unit test asserting take reverts when settlementFee > offerPrice for buy offers.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take / Midnight.settlementFee (buy offer, offerPrice == settlementFee)] Can a taker take a SetterRatifier-authorized buy offer where offerPrice == settlementFee exactly, causing sellerPrice = 0 and sellerAssets = units.mulDivDown(0, WAD) = 0, so the seller (taker) receives zero assets while the buyer (maker) pays units.mulDivDown(offerPrice, WAD) > 0, with the entire payment going to claimableSettlementFee, effectively charging the full price as a fee and giving the seller nothing? Preconditions: offer.buy == true; offerPrice == settlementFee; units > 0. Call sequence: setIsRootRatified -> take(buyOffer, tick=T where tickToPrice(T)==fee, ...). Invariant tested: sellerAssets must be > 0 when units > 0 and offerPrice > settlementFee. Scoped impact: settlement fee overclaimed (100% of payment); seller receives nothing. Proof idea: unit test at offerPrice == settlementFee asserting sellerAssets == 0.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/SetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Fee mis-accounting where settlement fee or continuous fee is overclaimed, underclaimed, unclaimable, or charged to the wrong side'] [Function: Midnight.take / continuousFeeCredit (multiple markets, same loanToken)] Can a feeClaimer call claimContinuousFee(marketA, amount, receiver) where amount > marketA.continuousFeeCredit but amount <= marketA.withdrawable, causing the subtraction _marketState.continuousFeeCredit -= amount to underflow and revert, but if the feeClaimer instead claims from marketB (same loanToken) which has higher continuousFeeCredit, the tokens transferred come from the shared loanToken balance, effectively claiming continuous fees from one market using another market's token balance? Preconditions: two markets share loanToken; marketA has low continuousFeeCredit but high withdrawable; marketB has high continuousFeeCredit. Call sequence: claimContinuousFee(marketB, marketB.continuousFeeCredit, receiver) [drains marketB's share of loanToken balance]. Invariant tested: claimContinuousFee must only transfer tokens that belong to the specified market. Scoped impact: continuous fee overclaimed from wrong market. Proof idea: invariant test asserting balance[loanToken] >= sum(withdrawable[allMarkets]) + sum(continuousFeeCredit[allMarkets]) after any claim.
